headline: ExxonMobil CEO sees chance of higher oil prices as earnings dip
summary: US oil giants reported lower earnings Friday with accounting effects muting the benefits of the Middle East oil shock, as ExxonMobil's CEO cautioned that crude prices could still increase.While surging prices are ultimately expected to boost US oil company earnings, both ExxonMobil and Chevron suffered a significant hit in the first quarter because of how US accounting rules require the reporting of derivative contracts.

headline: Iran war pushes California gas over $6
summary: STORY: Americans celebrating this week's 100th anniversary of the historic Route 66 - a famed symbol of carefree road trips - are getting hit with sticker shock, especially in California.Drivers in the Golden State on Friday were paying upwards of six dollars a gallon for gas — the highest price in two years.The U.S. war in Iran war has brought the worst-ever disruption to global oil supplies, driving up gas prices worldwide and posing particular hardships for drivers in California, the most populous U.S. state with the most cars on the road.California also has some of the highest gas prices in the country because of strict emissions standards, high taxes and its reliance on imported petroleum.Separately on Friday, Exxon Mobil said its quarterly production was down nearly 8% from the prior quarter, largely due to ongoing disruptions to the shuttered Strait of Hormuz.That dropped Exxon's quarterly profit to its lowest level in 5 years.:: ArchiveAbout 20% of Exxon's oil and gas production is located in the Middle East, giving it one of the highest exposure rates among the major oil companies.:: ChevronBy contrast, Chevron, the No. 2 U.S. oil producer, said on Friday that less than 5% of its production comes from the Middle East.Even though it is less exposed to the war, Chevron reported its lowest profit in five years, partly linked to the company's financial hedges.:: ArchiveShares of Exxon and Chevron were both lower in Friday trading.

raw.headline: Iran war pushes California gas over $6
raw.summary: STORY: Americans celebrating this week's 100th anniversary of the historic Route 66 - a famed symbol of carefree road trips - are getting hit with sticker shock, especially in California.Drivers in the Golden State on Friday were paying upwards of six dollars a gallon for gas — the highest price in two years.The U.S. war in Iran war has brought the worst-ever disruption to global oil supplies, driving up gas prices worldwide and posing particular hardships for drivers in California, the most populous U.S. state with the most cars on the road.California also has some of the highest gas prices in the country because of strict emissions standards, high taxes and its reliance on imported petroleum.Separately on Friday, Exxon Mobil said its quarterly production was down nearly 8% from the prior quarter, largely due to ongoing disruptions to the shuttered Strait of Hormuz.That dropped Exxon's quarterly profit to its lowest level in 5 years.:: ArchiveAbout 20% of Exxon's oil and gas production is located in the Middle East, giving it one of the highest exposure rates among the major oil companies.:: ChevronBy contrast, Chevron, the No. 2 U.S. oil producer, said on Friday that less than 5% of its production comes from the Middle East.Even though it is less exposed to the war, Chevron reported its lowest profit in five years, partly linked to the company's financial hedges.:: ArchiveShares of Exxon and Chevron were both lower in Friday trading.

headline: The UAE’s OPEC Exit Changes the Oil Trade — Here’s the Stock to Buy Now
summary: Oil investors woke up to a very different market this morning. The UAE — OPEC’s fourth largest producer — officially exited OPEC+ after months of tension over production quotas and market strategy. That matters because OPEC’s power has always depended on coordination. Once major members start choosing national interests over cartel discipline, pricing power weakens. ... The UAE’s OPEC Exit Changes the Oil Trade — Here’s the Stock to Buy Now

headline: Exxon Stock Slips. CEO Warns ‘Full Impact’ of War is Coming.
summary: Exxon  is experiencing both pain and profit from the Iran war, and its first quarter results reflected that split on Friday.  The oil major beat Wall Street’s earnings targets, helping reassure investors that Exxon is weathering some of the damage from the conflict.  Oil prices are still trading in a range within historical norms, despite the fact that war has caused the largest supply shock ever, pulling more than 10 million daily barrels off the market.

headline: Profit for the biggest US oil companies declined in the first quarter, but only on paper
summary: Profit for the two largest oil companies in the U.S. tumbled during the first quarter, a three-month period in which the price of crude and gasoline rocketed higher.  It's a setback on paper only, however, the result of financial hedges that backfired after the U.S. and Israel launched attacks on Iran in late February.  Exxon Mobil and Chevron reported quarterly results on Friday, with adjusted profits for both companies topping Wall Street expectations.

It's a setback on paper only, however, the result of financial hedges that backfired after the U.S. and Israel launched attacks on Iran in late February.  Exxon Mobil and Chevron reported quarterly results on Friday, with adjusted profits for both companies topping Wall Street expectations.

headline: Exxon Stock Rises. Why Earnings Should Reassure Investors.
summary: Exxon Mobil  was rising Friday after the oil major beat Wall Street’s earnings targets, helping reassure investors that  Exxon  is weathering some of the damage it has taken from the Iran war.  Exxon reported first-quarter adjusted earnings of $1.16 a share as revenue rose 2.4% from a year ago to $85.1 billion.  Chairman and CEO Darren Woods said the results showed the company was “a fundamentally stronger company than it was just a few years ago, built to perform through disruption and across market cycles.”

Chairman and CEO Darren Woods said the results showed the company was “a fundamentally stronger company than it was just a few years ago, built to perform through disruption and across market cycles.”

headline: ExxonMobil’s (NYSE:XOM) Q1 CY2026 Sales Beat Estimates
summary: Integrated energy company ExxonMobil (NYSE:XOM) reported Q1 CY2026 results beating Wall Street’s revenue expectations, with sales up 2.4% year on year to $85.14 billion. Its non-GAAP profit of $1.16 per share was 15.1% above analysts’ consensus estimates.

headline: Exxon Mobil’s Angola Subsea Deal And What It Means For Long Cycle Growth
summary: ExxonMobil (NYSE:XOM) has awarded Subsea 7 a subsea EPCI contract for the Likembe Redevelopment 2.0 Project offshore Angola. The award covers engineering, procurement, construction, and installation activities for the deepwater redevelopment. The project expands ExxonMobil's operational footprint in West Africa and adds to its global project portfolio. For investors tracking NYSE:XOM, this new subsea EPCI contract in Angola is closely aligned with the company’s core upstream business,...
